masthead.dek: Twenty more questions, and the answer is rarely the first name that springs to mind. Each routes to a set you can walk — a program, a bowl, a stadium, a record — with a filter or twist that knocks out the obvious. Narrow the field, place your bet, then tap to check.
masthead.footer_html: Twenty questions across programs, bowls, stadiums, the playoff era and records — every fact web-verified and sourced. Through the 2024-25 season.

dimensions: active coach wins at one school
plan.note: A second, fully distinct college-football run — none of these 20 questions overlap the first college-football set. Answers routed to right-sized sets (programs ~134, bowls ~40, stadiums ~50, records) and made non-obvious by reflex-defeaters and slicers. Michigan appears as an answer here even though it is used in the U.S. states set — answers may repeat across games, only questions may not.

questions.q: Which college football program has won more games than any other in the sport's history?
questions.detail: Michigan leads all FBS programs with about 1,012 all-time victories, roughly 34 ahead of second-place Ohio State.
questions.why: Longevity plus winning — Michigan began in 1879 and was the first team to top 1,000 wins.
questions.more: Ohio State (~978) and Alabama (~974) are the only others close.
questions.source: Yahoo Sports / CBS Sports all-time FBS wins (2025).

questions.q: Which school has finished more seasons undefeated than any other program?
questions.detail: Notre Dame has six undefeated seasons, the most of any program, ahead of Nebraska and Alabama with five each.
questions.why: Elite across many eras; Notre Dame's run reaches back to the Rockne years.
questions.more: Oklahoma and Ohio State are next with four apiece.
questions.source: NCAA.com / Yahoo Sports undefeated-seasons tallies.

questions.q: Which college has produced the most players taken first overall in the NFL Draft?
questions.detail: USC has had six No. 1 overall picks — Ron Yary, O.J. Simpson, Ricky Bell, Keyshawn Johnson, Carson Palmer, and Caleb Williams (2024).
questions.why: Top picks cluster at glamour programs; USC's tailback and quarterback factories pulled it clear.
questions.more: Notre Dame is second with five, stuck there since 1972.
questions.source: Sports Illustrated / AOL on USC's six No. 1 overall picks (2024).

questions.q: Setting aside the modern powerhouses, which school owns the most NCAA-recognized national championships of all time?
questions.detail: Yale holds 18 NCAA-recognized national titles, the most of any program — almost all from football's early decades.
questions.why: The slicer removes Alabama and Notre Dame and rewards the sport's first 50-year king.
questions.more: Alabama (16) and Princeton (15) follow.
questions.source: BestColleges / NCAA.com recognized-titles list.

questions.q: Which program has won the most Big 12 football championships?
questions.detail: Oklahoma won 14 Big 12 titles, the most in league history, before leaving for the SEC in 2024.
questions.why: Oklahoma was the Big 12's flagship for most of the league's existence.
questions.more: Texas is a distant second.
questions.source: Big12sports.com / Fox Sports Big 12 champions list.

questions.q: Since the College Football Playoff began, which team has earned a spot in the bracket the most times?
questions.detail: Alabama reached the CFP nine times in its first twelve years (2014–2025).
questions.why: The Saban-era machine made the field almost every season.
questions.more: Clemson and Ohio State tied for second with seven each.
questions.source: SI / NBC CFP appearance counts.

questions.q: Which bowl game is the oldest one still played today?
questions.detail: The Rose Bowl traces to a January 1, 1902 Michigan–Stanford game, the first postseason college football game.
questions.why: The 'Granddaddy of Them All' returned for good in 1916 and has run ever since.
questions.more: The Sugar, Orange, and Cotton bowls all began in the 1930s.
questions.source: Tournament of Roses / Library of Congress on the 1902 Rose Bowl.

questions.dimension: active coach wins at one school
questions.q: Among coaches still on the sideline, which Big Ten school employs the man who has won more games at a single league program than anyone in conference history?
questions.detail: Iowa's Kirk Ferentz passed Woody Hayes in 2025 for the most wins by a coach at one Big Ten program.
questions.why: Filtering to active coaches at one school points to the longest-tenured man standing.
questions.more: His 206th Iowa win passed Hayes's 205 at Ohio State.
questions.source: Eleven Warriors / Hawk Central (Sept 2025).

questions.q: Which school has produced the most winners of the trophy given to college football's best interior lineman?
questions.detail: Nebraska has nine Outland Trophy winners, including the only two-time winner, Dave Rimington.
questions.why: Nebraska's offensive-line tradition dwarfs everyone else's.
questions.more: Alabama is next with six.
questions.source: Wikipedia / CollegeVine Outland Trophy by school.

questions.q: Which team currently owns the nation's longest unbroken streak of reaching a bowl game?
questions.detail: Georgia has gone to a bowl in 29 straight seasons (1997–2025).
questions.why: The word 'currently' defeats the reflex to name Florida State, whose record 36-year streak ended in 2017.
questions.more: Oklahoma (27) and Alabama (22) trail.
questions.source: ESPN / cfbeveryday active bowl-streak list.

questions.q: The very first Heisman Trophy went to a player from which school — one that later dropped big-time football?
questions.detail: Jay Berwanger of the University of Chicago won the first award in 1935.
questions.why: Chicago was a national power under Amos Alonzo Stagg before leaving major football in 1939.
questions.more: Berwanger was also the first player ever chosen in the NFL Draft.
questions.source: Heisman.com / University of Chicago Athletics.

questions.q: Which school was the first to play its home games on a field that wasn't green?
questions.detail: Boise State installed the nation's first non-green field — its blue 'Smurf Turf' — in 1986.
questions.why: No school followed the idea for about two decades.
questions.more: Boise State later trademarked the blue-field look.
questions.source: BoiseDev / Boise State on the 1986 blue turf.

questions.q: Which team scored more total points in a single season than any other in the modern game?
questions.detail: The 2019 LSU Tigers scored 726 points, averaging 48.4 per game on the way to a 15-0 title.
questions.why: Guess high and recent — a 15-game schedule plus a record-setting offense.
questions.more: Joe Burrow's team rewrote records across the board.
questions.source: Sports-Reference / Bleacher Report on 2019 LSU's 726 points.

questions.q: Which SEC school set the league's all-time attendance record, packing in a crowd north of 109,000 for a 2004 game?
questions.detail: Tennessee's Neyland Stadium drew 109,061 against Florida in 2004, the largest crowd in SEC history.
questions.why: The slicer rewards the venue that actually packs in the most bodies, beyond its listed capacity.
questions.more: Neyland's listed capacity is about 101,915.
questions.source: Wikipedia / EssentiallySports on Neyland's record crowd.

questions.q: Setting the Big House aside, which school plays in the second-largest stadium in the country?
questions.detail: Penn State's Beaver Stadium seats about 106,572, second only to Michigan's 107,601.
questions.why: Removing No. 1 turns it into a real Penn State-versus-Ohio State guess.
questions.more: Ohio Stadium (~102,780) is third.
questions.source: SI / 247Sports on Beaver Stadium capacity.

questions.q: Which team became the first undefeated champion of a major conference ever left out of the College Football Playoff?
questions.detail: Florida State went 13-0 and won the ACC in 2023 but was left out of the four-team Playoff.
questions.why: A late quarterback injury drove the committee's call — the first such exclusion in Playoff history.
questions.more: The snub helped push the field to twelve teams the next season.
questions.source: ESPN / CNN on FSU's 13-0 2023 CFP exclusion.

questions.q: Which team most recently won the College Football Playoff national championship?
questions.detail: Ohio State beat Notre Dame 34-23 in January 2025 to win the first 12-team Playoff, for the 2024 season.
questions.why: Recency — the Buckeyes' first national title since the very first Playoff, a decade earlier.
questions.more: It was their ninth recognized national title overall.
questions.source: OhioStateBuckeyes.com / ESPN on the January 2025 title game.

questions.q: Alabama has gone to one particular bowl more than any other in its history — which one?
questions.detail: Alabama has appeared in 16 Sugar Bowls, more than any other bowl on its schedule.
questions.why: Geography and the SEC's historic Sugar Bowl tie route the Tide to New Orleans.
questions.more: The Orange and Cotton bowls are next with eight Alabama visits each.
questions.source: FanDuel / Bama Hammer on Alabama's 16 Sugar Bowl appearances.

questions.q: Which school has played in the Cotton Bowl more times than any other?
questions.detail: Texas has made a record 22 Cotton Bowl appearances.
questions.why: The Dallas game was a near-home bowl for the Longhorns for decades.
questions.more: No other team is close to that total.
questions.source: BroBible / Cotton Bowl history on Texas's record 22 appearances.

questions.q: A running back from which school holds the record for the most career rushing yards in major college football?
questions.detail: Wisconsin's Ron Dayne ran for 6,397 regular-season yards, still the FBS career record, winning the 1999 Heisman.
questions.why: A four-year workhorse on a run-first team is the perfect profile for the record.
questions.more: Counting bowl games, Dayne's career total tops 7,100 yards.
questions.source: Heisman.com / Wisconsin Badgers on Ron Dayne's record.
